title: alepidea longifolia subsp. swynnertonii
intro: Alepidea longifolia subsp. swynnertonii, also known as Swynnerton's alepid or Swynnerton's alepidea, is a plant species in the family Asparagaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate rheumatic and arthritic pain, and support the immune system. Alepidea longifolia subsp. swynnertonii is traditionally prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is used as a tincture or infused in oil for topical application.
